verbGlossary.CITED FOR: Record formally references a specific violation of {target} (e.g., safety violation log entry citing an OSHA Focus Four hazard category)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: Record is categorized using {target} taxonomy (e.g., accident log entry classified under an OSHA hazard category, quantity log entry classified under a MasterFormat division)
verbGlossary.INSPECTED AGAINST: {source} entry evaluates jobsite conditions against {target} required criteria (e.g., daily log inspection checked against OSHA hazard categories for fall protection compliance)
verbGlossary.LOGGED PER: Record is documented following {target} recordkeeping requirements (e.g., accident log entry recorded per OSHA 300 log format and retention rules)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED PER: Supporting field record is captured in the format required by {target} (e.g., safety violation documented per OSHA recordkeeping classification for incident reporting)
verbGlossary.CODED BY: Record is assigned a classification code from {target} (e.g., accident log coded by ANSI injury type, T&M labor entry coded by standard cost code)
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} or review is required and regulated by {target} (e.g., daily log inspection governed by International Building Code requirements at specific milestones)
verbGlossary.TESTED PER: Inspection entry follows {target} prescribed testing protocol (e.g., daily log inspection entry for fire suppression tested per NFPA standards)
verbGlossary.TRACKED FOR: {source} is captured specifically to support the target certification's credit requirements (e.g., waste log entry tracked for LEED construction waste management credit documentation)
verbGlossary.REPORTED UNDER: {source} is reported within the target certification's tracking framework (e.g., dumpster log entry reported under LEED Materials & Resources waste diversion requirements)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source} metric is compared against {target} industry threshold or published baseline (e.g., waste diversion rate benchmarked to LEED minimum diversion thresholds, actual production benchmarked to AACE earned value norms)
verbGlossary.VERIFIED AGAINST: {source} confirms compliance with {target} through independent verification (e.g., waste log entry verified against net-zero construction waste targets)
verbGlossary.MONITORED PER: {source}is tracked against {target} performance thresholds (e.g., equipment log entry monitoring fuel consumption per energy code commissioning requirements)
verbGlossary.ORGANIZED BY: {source} scope is structured according to {target} divisions or sections (e.g., manpower log organized by MasterFormat trade divisions, scheduled work organized by MasterFormat work sections)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source} operations metric is broken down by {target} for comparative analysis (e.g., productivity log segmented by MasterFormat division to compare trade output rates)
verbGlossary.CHARGED TO: {source}is allocated to a cost code in the target classification (e.g., timecard entry charged to {target} cost code for payroll and cost tracking)
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED AGAINST: {source} cost or budget quantity is measured relative to {target} breakdown structure (e.g., T&M ticket allocated against WBS code for change order substantiation)
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} is monitored over time using {target} breakdown structure (e.g., field productivity record tracked by WBS code to measure earned value progress)
verbGlossary.RECEIVED AGAINST: Delivery record documents material arrival checked against {target} submittal requirements (e.g., delivery log entry received against specified material and approved submittal)
verbGlossary.SHIPPED PER: {source}record references {target} governing material packaging, handling, and delivery requirements (e.g., material shipment shipped per Division 01 product delivery requirements)
verbGlossary.ISSUED UNDER: Plan revision is distributed referencing {target} it modifies (e.g., plan revision receipt issued under the affected specification section for field coordination)
verbGlossary.ESCALATED PER: {source}is formally escalated following {target} document's notice and claims procedures (e.g., delay log entry escalated per AIA A201 time extension provisions)
